suppress.notes: spring-security-core-6.5.9.jar: CVE-2018-1258 only applies when used with spring-framework 5.0.5, which is not in this project. Added: 2026-04-17

spring-security-crypto-6.5.9.jar: CVE-2018-1258 only applies when used with spring-framework 5.0.5, which is not in this project. The NVD CPE cpe:/a:pivotal_software:spring_security matches all spring-security artifacts including crypto, but the vulnerability is context-specific to framework 5.0.5. Added: 2026-04-20

spring-security-crypto-6.5.10.jar: CVE-2018-1258 only applies when used with spring-framework 5.0.5, which is not in this project. The NVD CPE cpe:/a:pivotal_software:spring_security matches all spring-security artifacts including crypto, but the vulnerability is context-specific to framework 5.0.5. Added: 2026-04-20
spring-security-core-6.5.10.jar: CVE-2018-1258 only applies when used with spring-framework 5.0.5, which is not in this project. Added: 2026-04-20

protobuf-java (all versions): cpe:2.3:a:google:protobuf:* and cpe:2.3:a:protobuf:protobuf:* are cross-language CPEs that NVD assigns to Google Protobuf CVEs regardless of which language binding is actually affected. They incorrectly match the Java protobuf-java artifact for Python-only and C++-only CVEs. The originating example is CVE-2026-0994, a recursion-depth counter bug in the Python protobuf library's json_format.ParseDict() function — code that does not exist in the Java implementation. Fix versions cited there (5.29.6, 6.33.5) are Python package versions.
This entry suppresses both cross-language CPE prefixes for any protobuf-java JAR (matched by filename via <filePath regex>). Genuine Java-implementation CVEs would be filed under the Java-specific cpe:2.3:a:google:protobuf-java:* CPE, which is NOT covered by this rule.
Implementation note: <filePath regex> + <cpe> (CPE 2.3 form, prefix match) is used rather than <packageUrl regex> + <cpe> (2.2 form) because the publish and nightly workflows run Dependency-Check with --disableCentral. Without the Central Analyzer, bare JARs in validation/target/dependency/ are not enriched with PURL identifiers, so a packageUrl matcher never fires. Vulnerability IDs in the report are emitted in CPE 2.3 form (cpe:2.3:a:...), and Dependency- Check's non-regex <cpe> matcher does a case-insensitive startsWith against that form. The trailing ':' in each <cpe> value distinguishes the cross-language 'protobuf' product from the Java-specific 'protobuf-java' product so the latter remains scannable. Added: 2026-05-22
